🌱 VERRA VCS ANALYSIS

Verra VCS Energy Registry
Data Quality Monitor

An independent data quality audit of 1,700 energy industry projects under the Verified Carbon Standard (VCS) — scoring completeness, validity, and integrity across key data fields to surface risks to carbon credit validation and market integrity.

Analysis Generated 81 countries · 92 methodologies
🔍 Single Project Lookup

Type to search — select a result to view full quality analysis
📐 How Quality Scores & Risk Classifications Are Calculated
Quality Score (0–100)

Each project is scored on the completeness and validity of its key data fields. Fields are weighted by their importance to carbon credit validation. A score of 100 means all fields are present and logically consistent.

FieldWeightWhy It Matters
Methodology20 ptsRequired for emission calculations
Registration Date15 ptsEstablishes project traceability
Crediting Period Start15 ptsDefines when credits can be issued
Crediting Period End15 ptsDefines credit validity window
Emission Reductions10 ptsCore metric for credit volume
Region Classification10 ptsGeographic classification integrity
Project Name5 ptsBasic identification
Project Proponent5 ptsAccountability traceability
Valid Date Order+5 ptsEnd date must be after start date
Risk Classification

Risk is assessed by combining the quality score with status-based flags. A project can be High Risk even with a decent quality score if its status indicates a terminal or critical lifecycle issue.

High Risk — quality score below 50, missing methodology, terminated status (Withdrawn / Rejected), or quality below 75 combined with 2+ risk flags
Medium Risk — quality score below 75, or "Late to Verify" status, or moderate data gaps (missing crediting period dates, missing region)
Low Risk — quality score 75 or above with no critical status flags and no significant missing fields
⚠ Important: Risk here refers to data quality risk, not project performance or financial risk.
